decisionSummary: Optional advisory compiler integration is designable as an advanced, explicit-user-action feature, but implementation must wait for a dedicated implementation sprint. The design copies a user-selected shader into a temp fixture, copies the source-backed Hyprland vertex shader into the same temp directory, runs glslangValidator only against temp files, and reports non-authoritative advisory results that cannot approve, block, or bypass production write safety decisions.

cleanupPolicyDefined.policy: Best-effort cleanup of the temp fixture directory after the advisory command completes. Cleanup failure is logged as an advisory cleanup warning and must not approve, block, or bypass any write.

missingToolPolicy.effect: If glslangValidator is missing, show that the optional advisory check is unavailable. Do not block writes, do not approve writes, and do not bypass the production screen-shader watchdog gate.

timeoutPolicy.effect: If the command times out, show an inconclusive advisory result. Do not block writes, do not approve writes, and do not bypass the production screen-shader watchdog gate.

advisoryFailurePolicy.effect: If glslangValidator rejects the temp fixture shader, display an advisory warning and compiler output. Do not automatically block writes or change validators.

advisorySuccessPolicy.effect: If glslangValidator accepts the temp fixture shader, display an advisory pass. Do not claim Hyprland runtime safety and do not bypass the production screen-shader watchdog gate.

sourceBackedVertexPairingRule.rule: Use tex320.vert only when the temp-copied fragment begins with #version 320 es; otherwise use tex300.vert.

glslcRejectionReason: The previous feasibility proof checked glslc and did not choose it because the tested SPIR-V-oriented invocation rejected Hyprland-shaped OpenGL GLSL ES fixtures for requirements that are not Hyprland's OpenGL runtime compile path.

futureAdvisoryFlowDesign.userVisiblePurpose: Optional advanced advisory check. It may catch syntax/link-shape issues before the user chooses to proceed, but it is not Hyprland runtime safety proof.
futureAdvisoryFlowDesign.userConsentBoundary: The app must only read or copy a shader file after a direct user action such as selecting an advisory check command for that exact file.
tempCopySteps: Copy the explicit user-selected fragment shader into that temp directory.
Inspect only the temp-copied fragment prefix for #version 320 es.
Copy tex320.vert or tex300.vert from the bundled/source-backed Hyprland fixture into the same temp directory.
Run glslangValidator -l against the temp vertex and temp fragment paths.
Capture stdout, stderr, exit status, and timeout status as advisory result data.
Best-effort cleanup the temp directory and log cleanup failures as advisory warnings.
futureAdvisoryFlowDesign.originalUserPathPolicy: The original user path is used only as the source for the explicit copy step after consent. It is not passed to glslangValidator.

recommendedValidationPolicy.summary: A future implementation may expose an optional advanced advisory check, but the check must not control write safety decisions and must not claim Hyprland runtime safety.

compatibilityGaps: glslangValidator is not Hyprland's live OpenGL driver, context, extension set, or linker implementation.
The advisory command can approximate Hyprland's vertex/fragment pairing but cannot prove behavior inside Hyprland's actual CShader::createProgram runtime path.
The advisory command does not prove uniform availability, sampler binding behavior, output behavior, GPU-specific compiler behavior, damage tracking requirements, or visual safety.
No official Hyprland non-live screen-shader validation interface was found.
The implementation proof sprint added a non-production helper for explicit-consent temp-copy handling, cleanup handling, and timeout handling, but UI rendering of advisory output is not proven.
proofUsed: Existing production gate approval report proves decoration.screen_shader remains writable with production gate enforcement intact.
Existing advisory compiler feasibility report proves glslangValidator fixture/temp command shape accepted good ES 300/320 fixtures and rejected invalid fixtures.
Official Hyprland OpenGL source inspection proves the vertex-pairing rule and runtime OpenGL compile/link path.

Report and test design records that advisory output is non-authoritative and cannot change validators, pending changes, write planning, apply flow, SAFE_WRITABLE_ROWS, write allowlist, or row enablement.
proofStillMissing: No UI proof exists for explicit user consent and advisory output rendering.
No production compile-aware validator implementation exists.
No proof exists that glslangValidator output is equivalent to Hyprland's OpenGL runtime compile/link behavior.
No official Hyprland non-live validation interface was found.
nextRecommendedSprint: Screen shader optional advisory UI exposure design sprint: design how to expose the non-production advisory helper as an explicit advanced user action without wiring advisory output into validators, pending changes, write planning, apply flow, or write safety decisions.
